steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts “average ratings can be misleading when only a few users have rated” with “over 8,000 users,” positioning the large sample size as a reason to “feel confident” the rating is reliable. The blank must express that 8,000 is a sufficiently large number.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
”Adequate” directly conveys that 8,000 users is a sufficiently large sample to justify confidence in the rating's reliability.

The other three either describe excess, focus on precision rather than quantity, or express insufficiency—none fit the passage's logic.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
